South Korea Inflation Accelerated to 26-Month High in May
Debt-fueled bets turbocharge South Korean stock-market returns as Kospi jumps 8%
Goldman Sachs says AI and energy resilience are creating a North-South divide in Asian markets
Exchange Traded Concepts Announces Launch of Hanwha Asset Management's PLUS Korea Manufacturing Core Alliance Index ETF (KMCA) on the U.S. Market

holdings.infoBox: KMCA is an equity ETF with a total of 36 individual holdings. The top holdings are Samsung Electro-Mechanics stock at 12.58%, SK hynix at 11.58%, Samsung Electronics at 8.39%, DAEDUCK ELECTRONICS at 5.90%, and HANMI Semiconductor at 4.29%.

overview.description: The PLUS Korea Manufacturing Core Alliance Index ETF (KMCA) is an exchange-traded fund that mostly invests in stocks based on a particular theme. The fund tracks a tier-weighted index of South Korean companies that derive at least 50% of their revenue from six sectors within the countrys manufacturing industry. The portfolio is weighted to emphasize industrial exposure, enhancing diversification. KMCA was launched on May 6, 2026 and is issued by PLUS.
infoTable: Akros Korea Manufacturing Core Alliance Index

data.title: South Korea Inflation Accelerated to 26-Month High in May
data.text: The benchmark consumer-price index rose 3.1% from a year earlier in May, reflecting the effects of higher oil prices amid Middle East tensions and the won's weakness against the U.S. dollar.

data.text: Investors are focusing on AI developments in North Asia. China is coming out of more than three years of deflation measured by the producer price index.
